zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Annable於1540年出生在英國英格蘭戴文普萊姆斯代文波特時，她的父親John Annable46歲，母親Anne Whipple17歲。她於大約1569年與William Hill在英國戴文艾可謝特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1627年在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃享年87歲。

nameMeanings.name: Annable
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire): variant of Amable , a derivative of the Old French and Middle English female personal name Amabil, Amabel, from Latin amabilis ‘lovable’. In some cases it may also be also a habitational name from Amble, Northumberland, recorded as Amble from 1203 onward but also as Anebelle (1292) and Anebill (1347).

variants.name: Hannibal
meanings: German: from a post-humanist personal name, Hannibal (see 2 below).

English: post-medieval variant of Annable , from Middle English Amabil, Annabel. This female personal name fell out of fashion in the late Middle Ages, and the source of the surname was no longer recognized. It was re-interpreted in the 16th century by classically educated gentlemen as being the name of the Carthaginian general, Hannibal (247–182 BC ). The surname was also sometimes later re-etymologized as honey + ball or bell, hence spellings such as Honeyball and Hunnibell. In this new guise, it coincided with an established variant of Annable with initial H-.

History: There is no evidence for the use of Hannibal as a personal name in England before 1619, when Hannibal Gammon was rector of Mawgan in Pyder, Cornwall. As a medieval surname, it was borne by Matthew Hanibal (1255) and Peter Haniballus, both described as ‘civis Romanus’, and obviously Italians, but it is unlikely that either of these Italian merchants or moneylenders founded an English family.

variants.name: Amable
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French and Middle English female personal name Amabil, Amabel, Latin Amabilis ‘lovable’. The Normans frequently made use of the Old French short form Mabile or Mabel (see Mable ) and, by dissimilation of m to n, developed a new form, Anabel, which gave rise to surnames such as Annable and Hannibal .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Annable was born in 1540, in Devonport, Plymouth, Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Annable, was 46 and her mother, Anne Whipple, was 17. She married William Hill about 1569, in Exeter, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1627, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 87.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Annable(이)가 1540년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 플림우스 데폰포트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Annable(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Whipple(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1569년 영국 데폰 세터에서 William Hill(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1627년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 87세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Annable родилась в 1540 г. в местности девонпорт, плимоут, девон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Annable было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Whipple, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Hill около 1569 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1627 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 87 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Annable nació en 1540, en Devonport, Plymouth, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Annable, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Anne Whipple, tenía 17 años. Se casó con William Hill aproximadamente 1569, en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1627, en Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 87 años.
